have hax : ([# "x"] : List Tp) ⇒ # "x" := Sequent.ax
rcases h (s := "x") (A := # "x") hax with ⟨Δ, hΔ, hEq | hEq⟩
· have hne : Δ ≠ [] := nonempty_premises hΔ
  cases Δ with
  | nil => exact hne rfl
  | cons A Γ =>
      simp at hEq
· have hne : Δ ≠ [] := nonempty_premises hΔ
  cases Δ with
  | nil => exact hne rfl
  | cons A Γ =>
      simp at hEq

def atom_count : Tp → Nat
  | Tp.atom _ => 1
  | Tp.ldiv A B => atom_count A + atom_count B
  | Tp.rdiv A B => atom_count A + atom_count B

def atom_count_list : List Tp → Nat
  | [] => 0
  | A :: Γ => atom_count A + atom_count_list Γ

intro h
let p : Tp := #"p"
let bad : Tp := p ⧸ (p ⧹ p)
have hp : [p] ⇒ p := Sequent.ax
have hldiv : [p, p ⧹ p] ⇒ p := by
  simpa [p] using
    (Sequent.ldiv_l (Δ := [p]) (A := p) (Γ := []) (B := p) (Λ := []) (C := p) hp hp)
have hseq : [p] ⇒ bad := by
  have hne : [p] ≠ [] := by simp
  simpa [bad, p] using
    (Sequent.rdiv_r (Γ := [p]) (A := p ⧹ p) (B := p) hne hldiv)
have hle : atom_count bad ≤ atom_count_list [p] := h hseq
norm_num [bad, p, atom_count, atom_count_list] at hle

For the leaf-count definitions above, `atom_count A = 3` and `atom_count_list Γ = 1`, so the claimed inequality becomes `3 ≤ 1`, impossible.

def occurs_atom (t : String) : Tp → Prop
  | Tp.atom name => name = t
  | Tp.ldiv A B => occurs_atom t A ∨ occurs_atom t B
  | Tp.rdiv A B => occurs_atom t A ∨ occurs_atom t B

intro h
let p : Tp := #"p"
let q : Tp := #"q"
let bad : Tp := q ⧸ (p ⧹ q)
have hp : [p] ⇒ p := Sequent.ax
have hq : [q] ⇒ q := Sequent.ax
have hldiv : [p, p ⧹ q] ⇒ q := by
  simpa [p, q] using
    (Sequent.ldiv_l (Δ := [p]) (A := p) (Γ := []) (B := q) (Λ := []) (C := q) hp hq)
have hseq : [p] ⇒ bad := by
  have hne : [p] ≠ [] := by simp
  simpa [bad, p, q] using
    (Sequent.rdiv_r (Γ := [p]) (A := p ⧹ q) (B := q) hne hldiv)
have hocc : occurs_atom "q" bad := by
  simp [occurs_atom, bad, p, q]
rcases h (Γ := [p]) (A := bad) (t := "q") hseq hocc with ⟨B, hB, hBt⟩
simp [p] at hB
subst hB
simp [occurs_atom] at hBt

There is a derivation `[#"p"] ⇒ A`: first derive `[#"p", (#"p" ⧹ #"q")] ⇒ #"q"` by `Sequent.ldiv_l` from the axioms `[#"p"] ⇒ #"p"` and `[#"q"] ⇒ #"q"`, then apply `Sequent.rdiv_r`. Also `occurs_atom "q" A` holds by definition. But any `B ∈ Γ` must be `#"p"`, and `occurs_atom "q" (#"p")` is false. Hence the existential conclusion fails.

op_000024.proof_sketch: Prove the forward direction by induction on a derivation of `[A] ⇒ C`. lemma support_ok_replace
    {Γ L R Λ : List Tp} {B B' C : Tp}
    (hocc : ∀ s : String, occurs_atom s B → occurs_atom s B')
    (h : support_ok (Γ ++ [B] ++ Λ) C) :
    support_ok (Γ ++ L ++ [B'] ++ R ++ Λ) C := by
  induction C generalizing Γ L R Λ with
  | atom s =>
      rcases h with ⟨X, hX, hXocc⟩
      simp at hX
      rcases hX with hX | rfl | hX
      · refine ⟨X, ?_, hXocc⟩
        simp [hX]
      · refine ⟨B', ?_, hocc s hXocc⟩
        simp
      · refine ⟨X, ?_, hXocc⟩
        simp [hX]
  | ldiv D C ihD ihC =>
      rcases h with ⟨_, hC⟩
      constructor
      · simp
      · simpa [List.append_assoc] using
          ihC (Γ := [D] ++ Γ) (L := L) (R := R) (Λ := Λ) (by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hC)
  | rdiv C D ihC ihD =>
      rcases h with ⟨_, hC⟩
      constructor
      · simp
      · simpa [List.append_assoc] using
          ihC (Γ := Γ) (L := L) (R := R) (Λ := Λ ++ [D]) (by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hC)

lemma support_ok_self_singleton (A : Tp) : support_ok [A] A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ⟨# s, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom]⟩
  | ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
      constructor
      · simp
      · simpa using
          (support_ok_replace
            (Γ := []) (L := [B]) (R := []) (Λ := [])
            (B := C) (B' := B ⧹ C) (C := C)
            (fun s hs => by simp [occurs_atom, hs]) ihC)
  | rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
      constructor
      · simp
      · simpa using
          (support_ok_replace
            (Γ := []) (L := []) (R := [B]) (Λ := [])
            (B := C) (B' := C ⧸ B) (C := C)
            (fun s hs => by simp [occurs_atom, hs]) ihC)

induction h with

| rdiv_r hne hBA ih =>
    exact ⟨hne, by simpa using ih⟩
| ldiv_r hne hAB ih =>
    exact ⟨hne, by simpa using ih⟩
| rdiv_l d_arg d_main ih_arg ih_main =>
    rename_i Δ A' Γ' B Λ C

def support_ok (Γ : List Tp) : Tp → Prop

  induction C generalizing Γ L R Λ with
  | atom s =>

      rcases hX with hX | rfl | hX
      · refine ⟨X, ?_, hXocc⟩
        simp [hX]
      · refine ⟨B', ?_, hocc s hXocc⟩
        simp
      · refine ⟨X, ?_, hXocc⟩
        simp [hX]
  | ldiv D C ihD ihC =>
      rcases h with ⟨_, hC⟩

      · simp
      · simpa [List.append_assoc] using
          ihC (Γ := [D] ++ Γ) (L := L) (R := R) (Λ := Λ) (by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hC)
  | rdiv C D ihC ihD =>
      rcases h with ⟨_, hC⟩
      constructor
      · simp
      · simpa [List.append_assoc] using
          ihC (Γ := Γ) (L := L) (R := R) (Λ := Λ ++ [D]) (by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hC)

            (Γ := []) (L := [B]) (R := []) (Λ := [])

op_000027.source_statement: Introduce an inductive proof predicate for sequents from atom-only contexts using only `ax`, `ldiv_r`, and `rdiv_r`, and prove: for all `{Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}`, `(∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → ((Γ ⇒ A) ↔ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation Γ A)`.
op_000027.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → ((Γ ⇒ A) ↔ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation Γ A)
op_000027.statement_formalization_notes: The prelude defines the intended left-rule-free derivation predicate with exactly the constructors `ax`, `ldiv_r`, and `rdiv_r`; the atom-only restriction is kept as the theorem hypothesis on the initial context.

op_000027.proof_sketch: Instantiate the statement at Γ = [#"p"] and A = #"p" ⧸ (#"p" ⧹ #"p"). The context hypothesis is immediate. The sequent [#"p"] ⇒ #"p" ⧸ (#"p" ⧹ #"p") is derivable in the full calculus: first derive [#"p", #"p" ⧹ #"p"] ⇒ #"p" by `ldiv_l` from two copies of `ax`, then apply `rdiv_r`. If the claimed equivalence held, this would give an `AtomOnlyNormalDerivation` of the same sequent. But since the succedent is a right division, such a derivation must end with `rdiv_r`, leaving a premise `AtomOnlyNormalDerivation [#"p", #"p" ⧹ #"p"] (#"p")`. Case analysis on that premise shows only `ax` could match the atomic succedent, and `ax` would force a singleton context, contradiction.
op_000027.proof_text:
intro h
let p : Tp := # "p"
let A : Tp := p ⧸ (p ⧹ p)
have hctx : ∀ x ∈ [p], is_atom x := by
  intro x hx
  rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hx with rfl
  simp [p, is_atom]
have hiff : ([p] ⇒ A) ↔ AtomOnlyNormalDerivation [p] A :=
  h (Γ := [p]) (A := A) hctx
have hseq : [p] ⇒ A := by
  apply Sequent.rdiv_r
  · simp
  · simpa [A, p] using
      (Sequent.ldiv_l
        (Δ := [p]) (A := p) (Γ := []) (B := p) (Λ := []) (C := p)
        (Sequent.ax : [p] ⇒ p)
        (Sequent.ax : [p] ⇒ p))
have hnorm : AtomOnlyNormalDerivation [p] A := hiff.mp hseq
cases hnorm with
| rdiv_r _ hinner =>
    cases hinner with
    | ax =>
        simp [A, p] at *

However, `AtomOnlyNormalDerivation Γ A` is impossible: any such derivation must end with `rdiv_r`, so it would require an atomic-succedent derivation on context [# "p", # "p" ⧹ # "p"], and case analysis shows that only `ax` could produce an atomic succedent, forcing a singleton context. op_000031.source_statement: Define what it means for a type and a context to lie entirely in the left-only fragment or entirely in the right-only fragment, and prove: if every formula in `Γ` lies in the left-only fragment, then `(Γ ⇒ # s)` in the full product-free calculus iff the corresponding translated context derives `# s` in the left-only calculus; likewise for the right-only fragment.

(∀ {Γ : List PFTp} {s : String},
  IsLeftOnlyCtx Γ →
    ((Γ ⇒ # s) ↔
      match leftCtx? Γ with
      | some ΓL => Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent ΓL (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.atom s)
      | none => False)) ∧
(∀ {Γ : List PFTp} {s : String},
  IsRightOnlyCtx Γ →
    ((Γ ⇒ # s) ↔
      match rightCtx? Γ with
      | some ΓR => Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Sequent ΓR (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Tp.atom s)
      | none => False))

A = some A_L` and `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.toProductFree A_L = A`, and similarly on lists `IsLeftOnlyCtx Γ` gives some `Γ_L` with `leftCtx? Γ = some Γ_L` and `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.ctxToProductFree Γ_L = Γ`. Then unfold `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent`; by definition it is just the full sequent on `Left.ctxToProductFree Γ_L`, so rewriting by that equality yields `(Γ ⇒ # s) ↔ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent Γ_L (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.atom s)`. Because `IsLeftOnlyCtx Γ` forces the translation to succeed, the `match leftCtx? Γ` expression cannot be `none`, so the stated left conjunct follows. The right conjunct is the same argument with `rightTp?`, `rightCtx?`, and the definition of `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Sequent`.

inductive AtomicCandidateTree : List Tp → String → Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      AtomicCandidateTree [# s] s
  | step_rdiv (Γ L Δ Λ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A)
      (hrec : AtomicCandidateTree (L ++ [B] ++ Λ) s) :
      AtomicCandidateTree Γ s
  | step_ldiv (Γ Γ₁ Δ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A)
      (hrec : AtomicCandidateTree (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R) s) :
      AtomicCandidateTree Γ s

intro Γ s
constructor
· intro h
  induction h with
  | base s =>
      exact Sequent.ax
  | step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hΓ : Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ) hc)
      subst Γ
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.rdiv_l (Γ := L) (Δ := Δ) (A := A) (B := B) (Λ := Λ) (C := # s) harg ih)
  | step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hΓ : Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R) hc)
      subst Γ
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.ldiv_l (Γ := Γ₁) (Δ := Δ) (A := A) (B := B) (Λ := R) (C := # s) harg ih)
· have hcomplete : ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (s : String), (Γ ⇒ # s) → AtomicCandidateTree Γ s := by
    intro Γ s
    let n := list_degree Γ
    have hmain : ∀ n (Γ : List Tp) (s : String), list_degree Γ = n → (Γ ⇒ # s) → AtomicCandidateTree Γ s := by
      intro n
      refine Nat.strong_induction_on n ?_

        | rdiv L B A Δ Λ =>

            have hlt : list_degree (L ++ [B] ++ Λ) < n := by
              have hΓ : Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ := by
                symm
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ) hc)
              rw [hΓ] at hdeg
              rw [← hdeg]
              simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
              omega
            exact
              AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg
                (ih (list_degree (L ++ [B] ++ Λ)) hlt (L ++ [B] ++ Λ) s rfl hrec)
        | ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R =>
            rcases hcases with ⟨harg, hrec⟩
            have hlt : list_degree (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R) < n := by
              have hΓ : Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R := by
                symm
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R) hc)
              rw [hΓ] at hdeg
              rw [← hdeg]
              simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
              omega
            exact
              AtomicCandidateTree.step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg
                (ih (list_degree (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R)) hlt (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R) s rfl hrec)
    exact hmain n Γ s rfl
  exact hcomplete Γ s

op_000036.source_statement: Define the least context-succedent closure generated by singleton self-support and the three sequent rules, and prove that it coincides with support_ok.
op_000036.formalized_statement: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ support_ok Γ A
op_000036.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the least closure as an inductive context-succedent predicate with singleton self-support, the two right-introduction propagation rules, and the occurrence-preserving replacement rule that subsumes the left rules.

op_000036.proof_sketch: Induct on `SupportClosure` for the forward implication, using `support_ok_self_singleton` and `support_ok_replace`. For the reverse implication, induct on the structure of `A`; the atomic case splits the witness membership in `Γ` as `L ++ [B] ++ R` and rebuilds the closure from `SupportClosure.self (# s)` via `replace`, while the division cases follow directly from the recursive clauses of `support_ok`.
op_000036.prelude_code:
inductive SupportClosure : List Tp → Tp → Prop where
  | self (A : Tp) :
      SupportClosure [A] A
  | ldiv_r (Γ : List Tp) (B C : Tp)
      (hΓ : Γ ≠ [])
      (h : SupportClosure ([B] ++ Γ) C) :
      SupportClosure Γ (B ⧹ C)
  | rdiv_r (Γ : List Tp) (C B : Tp)
      (hΓ : Γ ≠ [])
      (h : SupportClosure (Γ ++ [B]) C) :
      SupportClosure Γ (C ⧸ B)
  | replace (Γ L R Λ : List Tp) (B B' C : Tp)
      (hocc : ∀ s : String, occurs_atom s B → occurs_atom s B')
      (h : SupportClosure (Γ ++ [B] ++ Λ) C) :
      SupportClosure (Γ ++ L ++ [B'] ++ R ++ Λ) C
op_000036.proof_text:
intro Γ A
constructor
· intro h
  induction h with
  | self A =>
      simpa using support_ok_self_singleton A
  | ldiv_r Γ B C hΓ h ih =>
      exact ⟨hΓ, by simpa using ih⟩
  | rdiv_r Γ C B hΓ h ih =>
      exact ⟨hΓ, by simpa using ih⟩
  | replace Γ L R Λ B B' C hocc h ih =>
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using support_ok_replace hocc ih
· revert Γ
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      intro Γ h
      rcases h with ⟨B, hB, hocc⟩
      have hsplit : ∃ L R, Γ = L ++ [B] ++ R := by
        induction Γ with
        | nil =>
            cases hB
        | cons X Γ ih =>
            simp at hB
            rcases hB with rfl | hB
            · exact ⟨[], Γ, by simp⟩
            · rcases ih hB with ⟨L, R, hLR⟩
              exact ⟨X :: L, R, by simp [hLR]⟩
      rcases hsplit with ⟨L, R, rfl⟩
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (SupportClosure.replace
          (Γ := []) (L := L) (R := R) (Λ := [])
          (B := # s) (B' := B) (C := # s)
          (fun t ht => by
            simp [occurs_atom] at ht
            simpa [ht] using hocc)
          (SupportClosure.self (# s)))
  | ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
      intro Γ h
      rcases h with ⟨hΓ, hC⟩
      exact SupportClosure.ldiv_r Γ B C hΓ (ihC ([B] ++ Γ) hC)
  | rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
      intro Γ h
      rcases h with ⟨hΓ, hC⟩
      exact SupportClosure.rdiv_r Γ C B hΓ (ihC (Γ ++ [B]) hC)

op_000038.source_statement: Define an inductive predicate `CandidateTree : List Tp → Tp → Prop` by combining the new atomic-case constructors with one constructor for each right-introduction rule, and prove `CandidateTree Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)` for all `Γ` and `A`.
op_000038.formalized_statement: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), CandidateTree Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)
op_000038.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized `CandidateTree` by reusing the atomic constructors from `AtomicCandidateTree` and adding recursive constructors mirroring `ldiv_r` and `rdiv_r`.

op_000038.proof_sketch: Forward by induction on `CandidateTree`. Reverse by converting atomic sequents through `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030`, then inducting on the succedent and using `ldiv_invertible`/`rdiv_invertible` for division cases.
op_000038.prelude_code:
inductive CandidateTree : List Tp → Tp → Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      CandidateTree [# s] (# s)
  | step_rdiv (Γ L Δ Λ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A)
      (hrec : CandidateTree (L ++ [B] ++ Λ) (# s)) :
      CandidateTree Γ (# s)
  | step_ldiv (Γ Γ₁ Δ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A)
      (hrec : CandidateTree (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R) (# s)) :
      CandidateTree Γ (# s)
  | ldiv_r (Γ : List Tp) (A B : Tp)
      (hne : Γ ≠ [])
      (hrec : CandidateTree ([A] ++ Γ) B) :
      CandidateTree Γ (A ⧹ B)
  | rdiv_r (Γ : List Tp) (A B : Tp)
      (hne : Γ ≠ [])
      (hrec : CandidateTree (Γ ++ [A]) B) :
      CandidateTree Γ (B ⧸ A)
op_000038.proof_text:
intro Γ A
constructor
· intro h
  induction h with
  | base s =>
      exact Sequent.ax
  | step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hΓ : Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ) hc)
      subst Γ
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.rdiv_l (Γ := L) (Δ := Δ) (A := A) (B := B) (Λ := Λ) (C := # s) harg ih)
  | step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hΓ : Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R) hc)
      subst Γ
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.ldiv_l (Γ := Γ₁) (Δ := Δ) (A := A) (B := B) (Λ := R) (C := # s) harg ih)
  | ldiv_r Γ A B hne hrec ih =>
      exact Sequent.ldiv_r hne ih
  | rdiv_r Γ A B hne hrec ih =>
      exact Sequent.rdiv_r hne ih
· have hatom : ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (s : String), AtomicCandidateTree Γ s → CandidateTree Γ (# s) := by
    intro Γ s h
    induction h with
    | base s =>
        simpa using CandidateTree.base s
    | step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
        exact CandidateTree.step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg ih
    | step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
        exact CandidateTree.step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg ih
  have hcomplete : ∀ (A : Tp) (Γ : List Tp), (Γ ⇒ A) → CandidateTree Γ A := by
    intro A
    induction A with
    | atom s =>
        intro Γ h
        exact hatom Γ s ((thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 Γ s).2 h)
    | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
        intro Γ h
        have hne : Γ ≠ [] := nonempty_premises h
        have hinner : [A] ++ Γ ⇒ B := ldiv_invertible h
        exact CandidateTree.ldiv_r Γ A B hne (ihB ([A] ++ Γ) hinner)
    | rdiv B A ihB ihA =>
        intro Γ h
        have hne : Γ ≠ [] := nonempty_premises h
        have hinner : Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B := rdiv_invertible h
        exact CandidateTree.rdiv_r Γ A B hne (ihB (Γ ++ [A]) hinner)
  exact hcomplete A Γ

op_000041.source_statement: Prove exact completeness of the support closure by showing `∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)`.
op_000041.formalized_statement: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)
op_000041.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the stated exact-completeness theorem, using the repository's existing `Tp`, `SupportClosure`, and `⇒` notation.

op_000041.proof_sketch: Refute the universal equivalence by specializing to Γ = [#"p" ⧹ #"p"] and A = #"p". The `SupportClosure` side holds by starting from `SupportClosure.self (#"p")` and applying `SupportClosure.replace` to swap `#"p"` for `#"p" ⧹ #"p"`, since every occurrence of `"p"` in `#"p"` also occurs in `#"p" ⧹ #"p"`. If the claimed equivalence held, we would get `[#"p" ⧹ #"p"] ⇒ #"p"`. Then `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` forces `#"p" ⧹ #"p" = #"p"`, contradiction.
op_000041.proof_text:
intro h
let bad : Tp := #"p" ⧹ #"p"
have hsc : SupportClosure [bad] (#"p") := by
  simpa [bad] using
    (SupportClosure.replace
      (Γ := []) (L := []) (R := []) (Λ := [])
      (B := #"p") (B' := bad) (C := #"p")
      (fun s hs => by
        simp [occurs_atom, bad] at hs ⊢
        exact hs)
      (SupportClosure.self (#"p")))
have hseq : [bad] ⇒ #"p" := (h [bad] (#"p")).mp hsc
have heq : bad = #"p" :=
  (thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011 (A := bad) (s := "p")).mp hseq
simp [bad] at heq
op_000041.counterexample_text: Take Γ = [# "p" ⧹ # "p"] and A = # "p". Then `SupportClosure Γ A` holds via `SupportClosure.self (# "p")` followed by `SupportClosure.replace`, because any occurrence of `"p"` in `# "p"` also occurs in `# "p" ⧹ # "p"`. But `Γ ⇒ A` is impossible: by `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011`, a derivation `([# "p" ⧹ # "p"] ⇒ # "p")` would imply `# "p" ⧹ # "p" = # "p"`, contradiction.

op_000042.prelude_code:
lemma exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly {A : Tp} (hA : isLeftOnly A) :
    ∃ AL, toLeftTp? A = some AL ∧ Left.Tp.toProductFree AL = A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ⟨Left.Tp.atom s, ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      rcases hA with ⟨hA, hB⟩
      rcases ihA hA with ⟨AL, hAL, hAL_pf⟩
      rcases ihB hB with ⟨BL, hBL, hBL_pf⟩
      refine ⟨Left.Tp.ldiv AL BL, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [toLeftTp?, hAL, hBL]
      · simp [Left.Tp.toProductFree, hAL_pf, hBL_pf]
  | rdiv B A =>
      cases hA

lemma exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly {A : Tp} (hA : isRightOnly A) :
    ∃ AR, toRightTp? A = some AR ∧ Right.Tp.toProductFree AR = A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ⟨Right.Tp.atom s, ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | ldiv A B =>
      cases hA
  | rdiv B A ihB ihA =>
      rcases hA with ⟨hB, hA⟩
      rcases ihB hB with ⟨BR, hBR, hBR_pf⟩
      rcases ihA hA with ⟨AR, hAR, hAR_pf⟩
      refine ⟨Right.Tp.rdiv BR AR, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [toRightTp?, hBR, hAR]
      · simp [Right.Tp.toProductFree, hBR_pf, hAR_pf]

lemma exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly {Γ : List Tp}
    (hΓ : ∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) :
    ∃ ΓL, toLeftCtx? Γ = some ΓL ∧ Left.ctxToProductFree ΓL = Γ := by
  induction Γ with
  | nil =>
      refine ⟨[], ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | cons x xs ih =>
      have hx : isLeftOnly x := hΓ x (by simp)
      have hxs : ∀ y ∈ xs, isLeftOnly y := by
        intro y hy
        exact hΓ y (by simp [hy])
      rcases exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly hx with ⟨xL, hxL, hx_pf⟩
      rcases ih hxs with ⟨xsL, hxsL, hxs_pf⟩
      refine ⟨xL :: xsL, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simpa [toLeftCtx?, hxL] using
          congrArg (fun t => t.bind fun ys => some (xL :: ys)) hxsL
      · simp [Left.ctxToProductFree, hx_pf]
        simpa [Left.ctxToProductFree] using hxs_pf

lemma exists_right_ctx_of_allRightOnly {Γ : List Tp}
    (hΓ : ∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) :
    ∃ ΓR, toRightCtx? Γ = some ΓR ∧ Right.ctxToProductFree ΓR = Γ := by
  induction Γ with
  | nil =>
      refine ⟨[], ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | cons x xs ih =>
      have hx : isRightOnly x := hΓ x (by simp)
      have hxs : ∀ y ∈ xs, isRightOnly y := by
        intro y hy
        exact hΓ y (by simp [hy])
      rcases exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly hx with ⟨xR, hxR, hx_pf⟩
      rcases ih hxs with ⟨xsR, hxsR, hxs_pf⟩
      refine ⟨xR :: xsR, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simpa [toRightCtx?, hxR] using
          congrArg (fun t => t.bind fun ys => some (xR :: ys)) hxsR
      · simp [Right.ctxToProductFree, hx_pf]
        simpa [Right.ctxToProductFree] using hxs_pf

· intro Γ A hΓ hA

op_000042.proof_sketch: The repair first reinstates the missing fragment declarations used by the fixed statement: `isLeftOnly`, `isRightOnly`, the partial translations, and the translated sequent predicates. Then it proves by structural induction that a left-only or right-only formula translates successfully and round-trips back to the original full formula, and lifts this to contexts by list induction. With those witnesses, unfolding `leftTranslatedSequent` and `rightTranslatedSequent` reduces each side to `Left.Sequent` or `Right.Sequent`, which are definitionally the full sequent relation after rewriting by the round-trip equalities.
op_000042.prelude_code:
def isLeftOnly : Tp → Prop
  | Tp.atom _ => True
  | Tp.ldiv A B => isLeftOnly A ∧ isLeftOnly B
  | Tp.rdiv _ _ => False

def isRightOnly : Tp → Prop
  | Tp.atom _ => True
  | Tp.ldiv _ _ => False
  | Tp.rdiv B A => isRightOnly B ∧ isRightOnly A

def toLeftTp? : Tp → Option Left.Tp
  | Tp.atom s => some (Left.Tp.atom s)
  | Tp.ldiv A B => do
      let A' ← toLeftTp? A
      let B' ← toLeftTp? B
      pure (Left.Tp.ldiv A' B')
  | Tp.rdiv _ _ => none

def toRightTp? : Tp → Option Right.Tp
  | Tp.atom s => some (Right.Tp.atom s)
  | Tp.ldiv _ _ => none
  | Tp.rdiv B A => do
      let B' ← toRightTp? B
      let A' ← toRightTp? A
      pure (Right.Tp.rdiv B' A')

def toLeftCtx? (Γ : List Tp) : Option (List Left.Tp) :=
  List.mapM toLeftTp? Γ

def toRightCtx? (Γ : List Tp) : Option (List Right.Tp) :=
  List.mapM toRightTp? Γ

def leftTranslatedSequent (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match (toLeftCtx? Γ, toLeftTp? A) with
  | (some ΓL, some AL) => Left.Sequent ΓL AL
  | _ => False

def rightTranslatedSequent (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match (toRightCtx? Γ, toRightTp? A) with
  | (some ΓR, some AR) => Right.Sequent ΓR AR
  | _ => False

lemma exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly {A : Tp} (hA : isLeftOnly A) :
    ∃ AL, toLeftTp? A = some AL ∧ Left.Tp.toProductFree AL = A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ⟨Left.Tp.atom s, ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      rcases hA with ⟨hA, hB⟩
      rcases ihA hA with ⟨AL, hAL, hAL_pf⟩
      rcases ihB hB with ⟨BL, hBL, hBL_pf⟩
      refine ⟨Left.Tp.ldiv AL BL, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [toLeftTp?, hAL, hBL]
      · simp [Left.Tp.toProductFree, hAL_pf, hBL_pf]
  | rdiv B A =>
      cases hA

lemma exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly {A : Tp} (hA : isRightOnly A) :
    ∃ AR, toRightTp? A = some AR ∧ Right.Tp.toProductFree AR = A := by
  induction A with
  | atom s =>
      refine ⟨Right.Tp.atom s, ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | ldiv A B =>
      cases hA
  | rdiv B A ihB ihA =>
      rcases hA with ⟨hB, hA⟩
      rcases ihB hB with ⟨BR, hBR, hBR_pf⟩
      rcases ihA hA with ⟨AR, hAR, hAR_pf⟩
      refine ⟨Right.Tp.rdiv BR AR, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [toRightTp?, hBR, hAR]
      · simp [Right.Tp.toProductFree, hBR_pf, hAR_pf]

lemma exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly {Γ : List Tp}
    (hΓ : ∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) :
    ∃ ΓL, toLeftCtx? Γ = some ΓL ∧ Left.ctxToProductFree ΓL = Γ := by
  induction Γ with
  | nil =>
      refine ⟨[], ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | cons x xs ih =>
      have hx : isLeftOnly x := hΓ x (by simp)
      have hxs : ∀ y ∈ xs, isLeftOnly y := by
        intro y hy
        exact hΓ y (by simp [hy])
      rcases exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly hx with ⟨xL, hxL, hx_pf⟩
      rcases ih hxs with ⟨xsL, hxsL, hxs_pf⟩
      refine ⟨xL :: xsL, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simpa [toLeftCtx?, hxL] using
          congrArg (fun t => t.bind fun ys => some (xL :: ys)) hxsL
      · simpa [Left.ctxToProductFree, hx_pf, hxs_pf]

lemma exists_right_ctx_of_allRightOnly {Γ : List Tp}
    (hΓ : ∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) :
    ∃ ΓR, toRightCtx? Γ = some ΓR ∧ Right.ctxToProductFree ΓR = Γ := by
  induction Γ with
  | nil =>
      refine ⟨[], ?_, ?_⟩ <;> rfl
  | cons x xs ih =>
      have hx : isRightOnly x := hΓ x (by simp)
      have hxs : ∀ y ∈ xs, isRightOnly y := by
        intro y hy
        exact hΓ y (by simp [hy])
      rcases exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly hx with ⟨xR, hxR, hx_pf⟩
      rcases ih hxs with ⟨xsR, hxsR, hxs_pf⟩
      refine ⟨xR :: xsR, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simpa [toRightCtx?, hxR] using
          congrArg (fun t => t.bind fun ys => some (xR :: ys)) hxsR
      · simpa [Right.ctxToProductFree, hx_pf, hxs_pf]
op_000042.proof_text:
constructor
· intro Γ A hΓ hA
  rcases exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly hΓ with ⟨ΓL, hΓL, hΓ_pf⟩
  rcases exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly hA with ⟨AL, hAL, hA_pf⟩
  simp [leftTranslatedSequent, hΓL, hAL, Left.Sequent, hΓ_pf, hA_pf]
· intro Γ A hΓ hA
  rcases exists_right_ctx_of_allRightOnly hΓ with ⟨ΓR, hΓR, hΓ_pf⟩
  rcases exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly hA with ⟨AR, hAR, hA_pf⟩
  simp [rightTranslatedSequent, hΓR, hAR, Right.Sequent, hΓ_pf, hA_pf]

op_000046.source_statement: Construct the finite state graph generated by all subformula-bounded candidate-tree decompositions of a fixed sequent, and prove that Γ ⇒ A holds exactly when the root state (Γ, A) is accepted by that graph.
op_000046.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  ∃ G : Finset CandidateGraphState,
    (Γ, A) ∈ G ∧
    (∀ q ∈ G, RootBoundedState Γ A q) ∧
    (CandidateGraphAccepts (FiniteCandidateGraphStep Γ A G) (Γ, A) ↔ (Γ ⇒ A))
op_000046.statement_formalization_notes: I encoded the graph as a finite state set together with a branching transition relation on lists of successor states, since candidate-tree decomposition is not unary. Subformula-boundedness is captured by RootBoundedState using both root-subformula containment and the root degree bound.

op_000046.proof_sketch: A plausible Lean route is to prove soundness of `CandidateGraphAccepts` by induction on `CandidateGraphAcceptsWithin`, then prove a stronger root-parameterized completeness statement by induction on a derivation, using helper lemmas that each decomposition step preserves `RootBoundedState` and that acceptance is monotone under enlarging `G`. From that one could choose the recursively collected finite graph in the derivable case and a singleton root graph in the non-derivable case. In the current repo, however, only `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038` is available; the needed boundedness, finitary-support, and monotonicity lemmas are all absent, so I do not have a defensible short Lean formalization for this theorem.

op_000046.proof_sketch: Start from `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038`: if `Γ ⇒ A`, choose a witness `h : CandidateTree Γ A`. Let `G` be the finite `Finset` of node labels `(Δ, C)` occurring in `h`; finiteness is by structural recursion on the tree. The only real bridge is to show every node state is `RootBoundedState Γ A`, and that follows by induction on node occurrence: `candidates_list_degree` identifies the principal context formula at each atomic expansion, the degree bound is preserved on children, and transitivity of `CandidateSubformula` lifts every child formula back to the root sequent. Then a second induction on `h` shows the root is accepted by `FiniteCandidateGraphStep Γ A G`, since each constructor of `CandidateTree` matches one clause of `CandidateGraphStepCore` and its children stay inside `G`. For the converse, prove soundness by induction on `CandidateGraphAcceptsWithin`: each accepted step reconstructs the corresponding sequent rule, so acceptance of `(Γ, A)` implies `Γ ⇒ A`. In the non-derivable case, take `G = {(Γ, A)}`; by the same soundness lemma that singleton graph cannot accept the root, so the displayed equivalence still holds.

op_000048.source_statement: Define a recursive handedness-sensitive closure for arbitrary succedents by combining the atomic exposure predicate with the two succedent-division clauses, and prove that for every Γ and A this refined closure is equivalent to Γ ⇒ A.
op_000048.formalized_statement: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)
op_000048.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the handed atomic exposure predicate using the existing `AtomicCandidateTree`, then wrapped it in a `support_ok`-style recursive closure for arbitrary succedents.

op_000048.proof_sketch: Induct on `A`. The atomic case is exactly `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030`. For `ldiv` and `rdiv`, unfold `HandedSupportClosure`; the forward direction uses the induction hypothesis with `Sequent.ldiv_r` or `Sequent.rdiv_r`, and the reverse direction uses `nonempty_premises` together with `ldiv_invertible` or `rdiv_invertible`.
op_000048.prelude_code:
def HandedSupportClosure (Γ : List Tp) : Tp → Prop
  | Tp.atom s => AtomicCandidateTree Γ s
  | Tp.ldiv B C => Γ ≠ [] ∧ HandedSupportClosure ([B] ++ Γ) C
  | Tp.rdiv C B => Γ ≠ [] ∧ HandedSupportClosure (Γ ++ [B]) C
op_000048.proof_text:
intro Γ A
induction A generalizing Γ with
| atom s =>
    simpa [HandedSupportClosure] using
      (thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 Γ s)
| ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨hΓ, hC⟩
      exact Sequent.ldiv_r hΓ ((ihC (Γ := [B] ++ Γ)).1 hC)
    · intro h
      refine ⟨nonempty_premises h, ?_⟩
      exact (ihC (Γ := [B] ++ Γ)).2 (ldiv_invertible h)
| rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨hΓ, hC⟩
      exact Sequent.rdiv_r hΓ ((ihC (Γ := Γ ++ [B])).1 hC)
    · intro h
      refine ⟨nonempty_premises h, ?_⟩
      exact (ihC (Γ := Γ ++ [B])).2 (rdiv_invertible h)

op_000045.source_statement: Define the collection of formulas occurring anywhere in a candidate tree, and prove a global subformula property: whenever Γ ⇒ A, there exists a candidate tree for Γ and A in which every intermediate formula is a subformula of some formula in Γ or of A.
op_000045.formalized_statement: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (Γ ⇒ A) → ∃ h : CandidateTree Γ A, CandidateTreeFormulas h ⊆ {B | RootBoundedFormula Γ A B}
op_000045.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized formulas occurring anywhere via explicit node labels in a `CandidateTree`, and bounded them by a root-relative subformula predicate over the original context formulas and succedent.

op_000045.proof_sketch: Use `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038` to obtain some `h : CandidateTree Γ A` from the given derivation. It is enough to prove the stronger lemma that every tree is root-bounded: `CandidateTreeFormulas h ⊆ {B | RootBoundedFormula Γ A B}` for every `h`; the smallest missing bridge is a transitivity lemma for `CandidateSubformula`, together with lifting lemmas from `c ∈ candidates Γ` using `candidates_list_degree`. Then induct on a node witness `CandidateTreeNode h Δ C`: the `root` case is immediate, and the `step_rdiv_arg`/`step_ldiv_arg` cases show each formula in `Δ` already lies in `Γ` while the new succedent `A` is a subformula of the principal formula in `Γ`. For `step_rdiv_rec`/`step_ldiv_rec`, apply the induction hypothesis to the recursive subtree and lift its root-bounded formulas back to the original root by transitivity, since the only new child-context formula is `B`, a subformula of the principal formula in `Γ`. The `ldiv_r_rec` and `rdiv_r_rec` cases are analogous: formulas inherited from the recursive tree remain bounded because `A` and `B` are subformulas of the new root succedent `A ⧹ B` or `B ⧸ A`, and the rest of the context is unchanged. Finally, apply this stronger lemma to the tree returned by `thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038`.

(∀ (Γ : List Left.Tp) (A : Left.Tp),
    ∃ e : LeftCandidateTree Γ A ≃ CandidateTreeObj (Left.ctxToProductFree Γ) (Left.Tp.toProductFree A),
      (∀ h, leftCandidateTreeSize h = candidateTreeObjSize (e h)) ∧
      (∀ h, candidateTreeObjSize h = leftCandidateTreeSize (e.symm h)))
  ∧

    ∃ e : RightCandidateTree Γ A ≃ CandidateTreeObj (Right.ctxToProductFree Γ) (Right.Tp.toProductFree A),

op_000054.proof_sketch: Define the forward maps by recursion on fragment trees: left `base/step_ldiv/ldiv_r` go to full `base/step_ldiv/ldiv_r` using `candidates_ldiv_mem` and `simpa [Left.Sequent]`, and symmetrically on the right with `candidates_rdiv_mem`. For the inverse, recurse on a full `CandidateTreeObj` whose indices are `Left.ctxToProductFree Γ` and `Left.Tp.toProductFree A` (or the right analogue). The smallest missing bridge is to show that every context piece and principal formula appearing in such a full-tree node is still one-sided; `candidates_list_degree` gives the exact decomposition of the translated context, and `thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042` together with `exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly` / `exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly` (or the right-hand versions) converts the stored full sequent proof back to the fragment sequent proof. op_000050.source_statement: Define the deterministic residual atomic state obtained from a sequent `(Γ, A)` by recursively peeling the outer succedent divisions of `A`, pushing each left- or right-division argument into the context and recording the required nonemptiness side conditions. Prove that `HandedSupportClosure Γ A` holds exactly when this peeling process ends in a residual state `(Δ, #s)` with `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`, and that `SupportClosure Γ A` holds exactly when the same residual state has `∃ B ∈ Δ, occurs_atom s B`.
op_000050.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔
    ∃ Δ s, residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) ∧ AtomicCandidateTree Δ s) ∧
  (SupportClosure Γ A ↔
    ∃ Δ s, residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) ∧ ∃ B ∈ Δ, occurs_atom s B)
op_000050.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the residual peeling as a recursive function returning `none` exactly when a required nonempty-context peel fails, and `some (Δ, s)` when the succedent reduces to the atom `#s` over residual context `Δ`.

op_000050.proof_sketch: Induct on `A`. In the atomic case everything reduces definitionally. In the division cases, split on whether `Γ` is empty; in the nonempty branch, `residualAtomicState` and `HandedSupportClosure` recurse on the same residual context, while the `SupportClosure` clause is transferred through `thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036`.
op_000050.prelude_code:
def residualAtomicState : List Tp → Tp → Option (List Tp × String)
  | Γ, Tp.atom s => some (Γ, s)
  | [], Tp.ldiv _ _ => none
  | Γ, Tp.ldiv B C => residualAtomicState ([B] ++ Γ) C
  | [], Tp.rdiv _ _ => none
  | Γ, Tp.rdiv C B => residualAtomicState (Γ ++ [B]) C
op_000050.proof_text:
intro Γ A
induction A generalizing Γ with
| atom s =>
    constructor
    · simp [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState]
    · simpa [support_ok, residualAtomicState] using
        (thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 Γ (# s))
| ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
    cases Γ with
    | nil =>
        constructor
        · simp [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState]
        · simpa [support_ok, residualAtomicState] using
            (thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 [] (B ⧹ C))
    | cons A Γ =>
        constructor
        · simpa [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState] using
            (ihC (Γ := [B] ++ (A :: Γ))).1
        · rw [thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036]
          simp [support_ok, residualAtomicState]
          rw [← thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 (B :: A :: Γ) C]
          exact (ihC (Γ := [B] ++ (A :: Γ))).2
| rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
    cases Γ with
    | nil =>
        constructor
        · simp [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState]
        · simpa [support_ok, residualAtomicState] using
            (thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 [] (C ⧸ B))
    | cons A Γ =>
        constructor
        · simpa [HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState] using
            (ihC (Γ := (A :: Γ) ++ [B])).1
        · rw [thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036]
          simp [support_ok, residualAtomicState]
          rw [← thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036 (A :: (Γ ++ [B])) C]

op_000055.source_statement: Define the canonical reconstruction that re-inserts the uniquely determined outer `⧹` and `⧸` succedent peels of `A` above an atomic residual witness, and prove that for every `Γ` and `A` it gives a bijection between `CandidateTree Γ A` and pairs `(Δ, s)` with `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`.
op_000055.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  ∃ reconstruct :
      { p : List Tp × String //
          residualAtomicState Γ A = some p ∧ AtomicCandidateTree p.1 p.2 } →
        CandidateTree Γ A,
    Function.Bijective reconstruct
op_000055.statement_formalization_notes: Encoded the target pairs `(Δ, s)` as a subtype of `List Tp × String` carrying both the `residualAtomicState` equation and the `AtomicCandidateTree` witness, and stated the result as existence of a bijective reconstruction into `CandidateTree Γ A`.

op_000055.proof_sketch: Define `reconstruct` by turning a residual witness into `HandedSupportClosure Γ A`, then into a sequent, then into `CandidateTree Γ A`. The residual-witness subtype is subsingleton because `residualAtomicState Γ A` is deterministic, and surjectivity comes from extracting a residual witness from any candidate tree via the same equivalence chain.
op_000055.proof_text:
intro Γ A
let S :=
  { p : List Tp × String //
      residualAtomicState Γ A = some p ∧ AtomicCandidateTree p.1 p.2 }
have hS : Subsingleton S :=
  ⟨fun x y => Subtype.ext (Option.some.inj <| x.2.1.symm.trans y.2.1)⟩
let reconstruct : S → CandidateTree Γ A := fun x =>
  (thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038 Γ A).mpr
    ((thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 Γ A).mp
      (((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 Γ A).1).mpr
        ⟨x.1.1, x.1.2, x.2.1, x.2.2⟩))
refine ⟨reconstruct, ?_⟩
constructor
· intro x y _
  exact hS.elim x y
· intro htree
  have hseq : Γ ⇒ A :=
    (thm_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000038 Γ A).mp htree
  have hhand : HandedSupportClosure Γ A :=
    (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 Γ A).mpr hseq
  rcases ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 Γ A).1).mp hhand with
    ⟨Δ, s, hres, hatom⟩
  let x : S := ⟨(Δ, s), ⟨hres, hatom⟩⟩
  refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
  exact Subsingleton.elim _ _

op_000051.source_statement: Define left-only and right-only analogues of `HandedSupportClosure` inside the one-sided fragment calculi, and prove that for any left-only sequent, respectively right-only sequent, the obvious translation preserves and reflects the handed closure.
op_000051.formalized_statement:
(∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) →
    isLeftOnly A →
    (HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure Γ A))
  ∧
  (∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) →
    isRightOnly A →
    (HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure Γ A))
op_000051.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced fragment-level handed closures on `Left.Tp` and `Right.Tp`, with translated predicates mirroring `leftTranslatedSequent` and `rightTranslatedSequent`.

op_000051.proof_sketch: Define fragment handed closures by recursion on `Left.Tp` and `Right.Tp`, prove each is equivalent to the corresponding fragment sequent by induction, then combine `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` with `thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042` and the left/right witness extraction lemmas.
op_000051.prelude_code:
def leftHandedSupportClosure (Γ : List Left.Tp) : Left.Tp → Prop
  | Left.Tp.atom s => AtomicCandidateTree (Left.ctxToProductFree Γ) s
  | Left.Tp.ldiv B C => Γ ≠ [] ∧ leftHandedSupportClosure ([B] ++ Γ) C

def rightHandedSupportClosure (Γ : List Right.Tp) : Right.Tp → Prop
  | Right.Tp.atom s => AtomicCandidateTree (Right.ctxToProductFree Γ) s
  | Right.Tp.rdiv C B => Γ ≠ [] ∧ rightHandedSupportClosure (Γ ++ [B]) C

def leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match toLeftCtx? Γ, toLeftTp? A with
  | some ΓL, some AL => leftHandedSupportClosure ΓL AL
  | _, _ => False

def rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  match toRightCtx? Γ, toRightTp? A with
  | some ΓR, some AR => rightHandedSupportClosure ΓR AR
  | _, _ => False

lemma leftHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent :
    ∀ (Γ : List Left.Tp) (A : Left.Tp),
      leftHandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ Left.Sequent Γ A := by
  intro Γ A
  induction A generalizing Γ with
  | atom s =>
      simpa [leftHandedSupportClosure, Left.Sequent, Left.Tp.toProductFree] using
        (thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 (Left.ctxToProductFree Γ) s)
  | ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
      constructor
      · rintro ⟨hΓ, hC⟩
        exact Left.Sequent.ldiv_r hΓ ((ihC (Γ := [B] ++ Γ)).1 hC)
      · intro h
        refine ⟨Left.nonempty_premises h, ?_⟩
        exact (ihC (Γ := [B] ++ Γ)).2 (Left.ldiv_invertible h)

lemma rightHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent :
    ∀ (Γ : List Right.Tp) (A : Right.Tp),
      rightHandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ Right.Sequent Γ A := by
  intro Γ A
  induction A generalizing Γ with
  | atom s =>
      simpa [rightHandedSupportClosure, Right.Sequent, Right.Tp.toProductFree] using
        (thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030 (Right.ctxToProductFree Γ) s)
  | rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
      constructor
      · rintro ⟨hΓ, hC⟩
        exact Right.Sequent.rdiv_r hΓ ((ihC (Γ := Γ ++ [B])).1 hC)
      · intro h
        refine ⟨Right.nonempty_premises h, ?_⟩
        exact (ihC (Γ := Γ ++ [B])).2 (Right.rdiv_invertible h)
op_000051.proof_text:
constructor
· intro Γ A hΓ hA
  rcases exists_left_ctx_of_allLeftOnly hΓ with ⟨ΓL, hΓL, _⟩
  rcases exists_left_tp_of_isLeftOnly hA with ⟨AL, hAL, _⟩
  calc
    HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ Γ ⇒ A :=
      thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 Γ A
    _ ↔ leftTranslatedSequent Γ A :=
      (thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042.1 Γ A hΓ hA)
    _ ↔ leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure Γ A := by
      simp [leftTranslatedSequent, leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure, hΓL, hAL,
        leftHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent]
· intro Γ A hΓ hA
  rcases exists_right_ctx_of_allRightOnly hΓ with ⟨ΓR, hΓR, _⟩
  rcases exists_right_tp_of_isRightOnly hA with ⟨AR, hAR, _⟩
  calc
    HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ Γ ⇒ A :=
      thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 Γ A
    _ ↔ rightTranslatedSequent Γ A :=
      (thm_full_one_sided_conservativity_000042.2 Γ A hΓ hA)
    _ ↔ rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure Γ A := by
      simp [rightTranslatedSequent, rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure, hΓR, hAR,
        rightHandedSupportClosure_iff_sequent]

op_000053.source_statement: Prove a cross-fragment rigidity theorem: if every formula in Γ is left-only and A is right-only, then Γ ⇒ A implies A is atomic; dually, if every formula in Γ is right-only and A is left-only, then Γ ⇒ A implies A is atomic.
op_000053.formalized_statement:
(∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) →
    isRightOnly A →
    (Γ ⇒ A) →
    is_atom A)
  ∧
  (∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) →
    isLeftOnly A →
    (Γ ⇒ A) →
    is_atom A)

op_000053.proof_sketch: Use `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` to turn `Γ ⇒ A` into `HandedSupportClosure Γ A`, then apply `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050` to obtain `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` together with `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`. If `A` is right-only and non-atomic, `residualAtomicState` only peels outer `rdiv`s, so `Δ` has the form `Γ ++ ΔR` where `ΔR` is a nonempty right-only suffix; also `Γ ≠ []` by derivability. Hence the mixed non-atomic case is impossible, so `A` must be atomic. The second conjunct is the exact left/right mirror of the same argument.

op_000061.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (Δ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
    (∀ B ∈ Δ, ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, subformula B C) ∧
    ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, occurs_atom s C

op_000061.proof_sketch: Prove it by induction on `A`, unfolding `residualAtomicState`; no heavy derived theorem is needed because the procedure just peels outer `⧹` and `⧸` constructors deterministically. If `A = # t`, then the equation forces `Δ = Γ` and `s = t`, so every `B ∈ Δ` already lies in `[A] ++ Γ`, and `occurs_atom s A` gives the atom witness. If `A = B ⧹ C`, the `Γ = []` branch is impossible since `residualAtomicState [] (B ⧹ C) = none`; otherwise the equation reduces to `residualAtomicState ([B] ++ Γ) C = some (Δ, s)`, and the induction hypothesis gives witnesses in `[C] ++ [B] ++ Γ`. Transport those witnesses to `[B ⧹ C] ++ Γ`: a witness formula coming from `Γ` is kept as is, while one coming from `C` or `B` becomes a subformula of `B ⧹ C`, and similarly any `occurs_atom s` witness in `C` or `B` lifts to `occurs_atom s (B ⧹ C)` by unfolding `occurs_atom`. The `A = C ⧸ B` case is identical, using the recursive call on `(Γ ++ [B], C)` and the corresponding one-step embeddings into `C ⧸ B`.

op_000061.source_statement: Prove a residual subformula theorem: if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)`, then every formula in `Δ` is a subformula of some formula in `Γ` or of `A`, and the atom `s` already occurs in `Γ` or `A`.
op_000061.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (Δ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
    (∀ B ∈ Δ, ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, TpSubformula B C) ∧
    ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, occurs_atom s C
op_000061.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a minimal local subformula relation because the current context has `occurs_atom` but no existing subformula predicate; “in Γ or of A” is encoded as membership in `[A] ++ Γ`.

op_000061.source_statement: Prove a residual subformula theorem: if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)`, then every formula in `Δ` is a subformula of some formula in `Γ` or of `A`, and the atom `s` already occurs in `Γ` or `A`.
op_000061.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ Δ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
    (∀ B ∈ Δ, (∃ C ∈ Γ, subformula B C) ∨ subformula B A) ∧
    ((∃ C ∈ Γ, occurs_atom s C) ∨ occurs_atom s A)
op_000061.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a local recursive `subformula` predicate because no repository-level subformula relation was present in the available context; reused existing `occurs_atom` for atom occurrence.

op_000061.source_statement: Prove a residual subformula theorem: if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)`, then every formula in `Δ` is a subformula of some formula in `Γ` or of `A`, and the atom `s` already occurs in `Γ` or `A`.
op_000061.formalized_statement:
∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {s : String},
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
    (∀ B ∈ Δ, ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C) ∧
    (∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], occurs_atom s C)
op_000061.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a minimal local `Subformula` relation because the current theory context provides `occurs_atom` but no existing subformula predicate.

op_000061.proof_sketch: Induct on `A`, unfold `residualAtomicState`, and in the division cases pass the induction-hypothesis witnesses from the residualized context to `Γ ++ [A]` by splitting the membership witness and lifting through the outer `⧹` or `⧸` constructor.
op_000061.prelude_code:
inductive Subformula : Tp → Tp → Prop where
  | refl (A : Tp) : Subformula A A
  | ldiv_left {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A B → Subformula A (B ⧹ C)
  | ldiv_right {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A C → Subformula A (B ⧹ C)
  | rdiv_left {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A B → Subformula A (B ⧸ C)
  | rdiv_right {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A C → Subformula A (B ⧸ C)
op_000061.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A s hres
induction A generalizing Γ Δ s with
| atom t =>
    simp [residualAtomicState] at hres
    rcases hres with ⟨rfl, rfl⟩
    constructor
    · intro B hB
      exact ⟨B, by simp [hB], Subformula.refl B⟩
    · exact ⟨# t, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom]⟩
| ldiv B C ihB ihC =>
    cases Γ with
    | nil =>
        simp [residualAtomicState] at hres
    | cons A Γ =>
        have hih := ihC (Γ := B :: A :: Γ) (Δ := Δ) (s := s) hres
        rcases hih with ⟨hsub, hocc⟩
        constructor
        · intro X hX
          rcases hsub X hX with ⟨Y, hYmem, hXY⟩
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYeq | hYmem | hYeq
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨B ⧹ C, by simp, Subformula.ldiv_left hXY⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨A, by simp, hXY⟩
          · exact ⟨Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hXY⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨B ⧹ C, by simp, Subformula.ldiv_right hXY⟩
        · rcases hocc with ⟨Y, hYmem, hYs⟩
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYeq | hYmem | hYeq
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨B ⧹ C, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨A, by simp, hYs⟩
          · exact ⟨Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hYs⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨B ⧹ C, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]⟩
| rdiv C B ihC ihB =>
    cases Γ with
    | nil =>
        simp [residualAtomicState] at hres
    | cons A Γ =>
        have hih := ihC (Γ := (A :: Γ) ++ [B]) (Δ := Δ) (s := s) hres
        rcases hih with ⟨hsub, hocc⟩
        constructor
        · intro X hX
          rcases hsub X hX with ⟨Y, hYmem, hXY⟩
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYmem | hYeq | hYeq
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨A, by simp, hXY⟩
          · exact ⟨Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hXY⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨C ⧸ B, by simp, Subformula.rdiv_right hXY⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨C ⧸ B, by simp, Subformula.rdiv_left hXY⟩
        · rcases hocc with ⟨Y, hYmem, hYs⟩
          simp [List.mem_append] at hYmem
          rcases hYmem with hYeq | hYmem | hYeq | hYeq
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨A, by simp, hYs⟩
          · exact ⟨Y, by simp [List.mem_append, hYmem], hYs⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨C ⧸ B, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]⟩
          · subst Y
            exact ⟨C ⧸ B, by simp, by simp [occurs_atom, hYs]⟩

op_000062.source_statement: Define the atomic residual transition graph on states `(Δ, s)` generated by one-step decompositions of `AtomicCandidateTree`, and prove finite-state recognition: `Γ ⇒ A` holds exactly when the initial state returned by `residualAtomicState Γ A` is accepted by that graph.
op_000062.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (Γ ⇒ A) ↔
    ∃ Δ s, residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) ∧
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)
op_000062.statement_formalization_notes: Conservatively formalized finite-state recognition as recognition by a residual-state graph, without adding a separate finiteness/cardinality clause.

op_000062.proof_sketch: First prove that `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)` is equivalent to `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s` by induction, since `AtomicResidualGraphStep.rdiv/ldiv` match `AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv/step_ldiv` exactly and the base cases coincide. Then combine this bridge with `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050` and `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` to move between sequents, handed support closure, and residual witnesses.
op_000062.prelude_code:
abbrev AtomicResidualState := List Tp × String

inductive AtomicResidualGraphStep : AtomicResidualState → AtomicResidualState → Prop where
  | rdiv (Γ L Δ Λ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A) :
      AtomicResidualGraphStep (Γ, s) (L ++ [B] ++ Λ, s)
  | ldiv (Γ Γ₁ Δ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A) :
      AtomicResidualGraphStep (Γ, s) (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R, s)

inductive AtomicResidualGraphAccepts : AtomicResidualState → Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts ([# s], s)
  | step {p q : AtomicResidualState}
      (hstep : AtomicResidualGraphStep p q)
      (hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q) :
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p
op_000062.proof_text:
intro Γ A
have hgraph_to_tree :
    ∀ p : AtomicResidualState,
      AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p → AtomicCandidateTree p.1 p.2
· intro p hacc
  induction hacc with
  | base s =>
      exact AtomicCandidateTree.base s
  | step hstep hacc ih =>
      cases hstep with
      | rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg =>
          simpa using AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg ih
      | ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg =>
          simpa using AtomicCandidateTree.step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg ih
have htree_to_graph :
    ∀ {Δ : List Tp} {s : String},
      AtomicCandidateTree Δ s → AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)
· intro Δ s htree
  induction htree with
  | base s =>
      simpa using AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.base s
  | step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      exact AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step
        (AtomicResidualGraphStep.rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg) ih
  | step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg hrec ih =>
      exact AtomicResidualGraphAccepts.step
        (AtomicResidualGraphStep.ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg) ih
constructor
· intro hseq
  have hhand : HandedSupportClosure Γ A :=
    (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 Γ A).mpr hseq
  rcases ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 Γ A).1).mp hhand with
    ⟨Δ, s, hres, htree⟩
  exact ⟨Δ, s, hres, htree_to_graph htree⟩
· rintro ⟨Δ, s, hres, hacc⟩
  have hhand : HandedSupportClosure Γ A :=
    ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 Γ A).1).mpr
      ⟨Δ, s, hres, hgraph_to_tree (Δ, s) hacc⟩
  exact (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 Γ A).mp hhand

op_000057.source_statement: Prove a quantitative residual-search bound: if `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s`, then every branch of the atomic search has length at most `list_degree Δ`, so after residualization the nondeterministic part of proof search is depth-bounded by the residual context alone.
op_000057.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (Δ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
  AtomicCandidateTree Δ s →
  ∀ n : Nat, AtomicBranchLength Δ s n → n ≤ list_degree Δ
op_000057.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized branch length with an indexed proposition `AtomicBranchLength Δ s n` counting recursive atomic decomposition steps; the explicit `AtomicCandidateTree Δ s` premise is kept to match the source statement.

op_000057.proof_sketch: Ignore the residual assumptions and prove the stronger fact by induction on `AtomicBranchLength`. In each step, `candidates_list_degree` identifies the ambient context, and `omega` closes the resulting one-step degree-gap inequality.
op_000057.prelude_code:
inductive AtomicBranchLength : List Tp → String → Nat → Prop where
  | base (s : String) :
      AtomicBranchLength [# s] s 0
  | step_rdiv (Γ L Δ Λ : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String) (n : Nat)
      (hc : Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A)
      (hrec : AtomicBranchLength (L ++ [B] ++ Λ) s n) :
      AtomicBranchLength Γ s (n + 1)
  | step_ldiv (Γ Γ₁ Δ R : List Tp) (A B : Tp) (s : String) (n : Nat)
      (hc : Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R ∈ candidates Γ)
      (harg : Δ ⇒ A)
      (hrec : AtomicBranchLength (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R) s n) :
      AtomicBranchLength Γ s (n + 1)
op_000057.proof_text:
intro Γ A Δ s _ _ n hlen
have hmain :
    ∀ {Δ : List Tp} {s : String} {n : Nat},
      AtomicBranchLength Δ s n → n ≤ list_degree Δ := by
  intro Δ s n h
  induction h with
  | base s =>
      simp [list_degree, tp_degree]
  | step_rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s n hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hΓ : Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ) hc)
      have hgap : list_degree (L ++ [B] ++ Λ) + 1 ≤ list_degree Γ := by
        rw [hΓ]
        simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
        omega
      exact le_trans (Nat.succ_le_succ ih) hgap
  | step_ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s n hc harg hrec ih =>
      have hΓ : Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R) hc)
      have hgap : list_degree (Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R) + 1 ≤ list_degree Γ := by
        rw [hΓ]
        simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
        omega
      exact le_trans (Nat.succ_le_succ ih) hgap
exact hmain hlen

op_000064.source_statement: If `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)`, then every state visited along any accepting run from `(Δ, s)` has the form `(Δ', s)` with every formula in `Δ'` a subformula of some formula in `Γ` or of `A`.
op_000064.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (Δ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
  AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s) →
  ∀ (Δ' : List Tp) (t : String),
    AtomicResidualGraphVisited (Δ, s) (Δ', t) →
      t = s ∧ ∀ B ∈ Δ', ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, TpSubformula B C
op_000064.statement_formalization_notes: Used a local `TpSubformula` relation and an `AtomicResidualGraphVisited` predicate to encode states occurring along accepting runs.

op_000064.proof_sketch: Prove first that `TpSubformula` is transitive; this is the smallest missing bridge, because a graph step introduces the new formula `B` only as an immediate subformula of the principal division formula already present in the parent context. Next, from `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)`, do induction on `A` (with the empty-context division cases impossible) to show the root invariant `∀ B ∈ Δ, ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, TpSubformula B C`; in the `ldiv` and `rdiv` cases, formulas coming from the recursive residual are lifted back to the original goal by `TpSubformula.ldiv_left/right` or `TpSubformula.rdiv_left/right`. Then show that `AtomicResidualGraphStep` preserves the stronger invariant `t = s ∧ ∀ B ∈ context, ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, TpSubformula B C`: `candidates_list_degree` rewrites the parent context as `L ++ [B ⧸ A'] ++ Δ ++ Λ` or `Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A' ⧹ B] ++ R`, so inherited formulas are handled by the parent invariant, while the new `B` is bounded by transitivity through the principal formula. Finally, induct on `AtomicResidualGraphVisited (Δ, s) (Δ', t)`; the root case gives `t = s` and the root subformula bound from the residual-state lemma, and the step case uses the one-step preservation lemma. This yields `t = s` and `∀ B ∈ Δ', ∃ C ∈ [A] ++ Γ, TpSubformula B C` as required.

op_000064.source_statement: If `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` and `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)`, then every state visited along any accepting run from `(Δ, s)` has the form `(Δ', s)` with every formula in `Δ'` a subformula of some formula in `Γ` or of `A`.
op_000064.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (Δ : List Tp) (s : String),
  residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) →
  AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s) →
  ∀ (p : AtomicResidualState),
    AtomicResidualVisitedOnAcceptingRun (Δ, s) p →
    ∃ Δ' : List Tp, p = (Δ', s) ∧ residualStateSubformulaBound Γ A (Δ', s)
op_000064.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a minimal recursive subformula predicate and a visit predicate for states lying on some accepting run.

op_000064.proof_sketch: First prove that `residualSubformulaOf` is transitive; that is the smallest missing bridge needed to lift recursive residual bounds through a peeled `⧹` or `⧸` formula. Then induct on `A` to show that `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` implies `residualStateSubformulaBound Γ A (Δ, s)`: in the atomic case `Δ = Γ`, and in the division cases the recursive bound for `[B] ++ Γ` or `Γ ++ [B]` is pushed back to the original goal by transitivity through the principal formula `B ⧹ C` or `C ⧸ B`. Next prove one-step preservation: if `residualStateSubformulaBound Γ0 A0 (ctx, s)` and `AtomicResidualGraphStep (ctx, s) (ctx', s)`, then `residualStateSubformulaBound Γ0 A0 (ctx', s)`; `candidates_list_degree` rewrites `ctx` as either `L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ` or `Γ1 ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R`, so carried formulas stay bounded and the newly exposed `B` is an immediate subformula of the principal division formula. Finally, induct on `AtomicResidualVisitedOnAcceptingRun (Δ, s) p`, threading the fixed second component and the bound from the initial residual state. This yields `p = (Δ', s)` for some `Δ'` together with `residualStateSubformulaBound Γ A (Δ', s)`; the explicit `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)` assumption is only needed for the `here` base case.

op_000059.source_statement: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is right-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`.
op_000059.formalized_statement:
(∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) →
    isLeftOnly A →
    (SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A))
  ∧
  (∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
    (∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) →
    isRightOnly A →
    (SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A))
op_000059.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized in the standard left/right conjunctive shape using the existing predicates `isLeftOnly`, `isRightOnly`, `SupportClosure`, and `HandedSupportClosure`.

op_000059.proof_sketch: Refute the conjunction using the left-only side. Take Γ = [#"p" ⧹ #"p"] and A = #"p". Every member of Γ is left-only, and A is left-only. `SupportClosure [#"p" ⧹ #"p"] (#"p")` holds by replacing `#"p"` with `#"p" ⧹ #"p"` in the singleton support witness. If the claimed equivalence held, we would get `HandedSupportClosure [#"p" ⧹ #"p"] (#"p")`, hence `[ #"p" ⧹ #"p" ] ⇒ #"p"` by `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048`. Then `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` forces `#"p" ⧹ #"p" = #"p"`, contradiction.
op_000059.proof_text:
intro h
let bad : Tp := #"p" ⧹ #"p"
have hleft : ∀ x ∈ [bad], isLeftOnly x := by
  intro x hx
  rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hx with rfl
  simp [bad, isLeftOnly]
have hA : isLeftOnly (#"p" : Tp) := by
  simp [isLeftOnly]
have hsc : SupportClosure [bad] (#"p") := by
  simpa [bad] using
    (SupportClosure.replace
      (Γ := []) (L := []) (R := []) (Λ := [])
      (B := #"p") (B' := bad) (C := #"p")
      (fun s hs => by
        simp [occurs_atom, bad] at hs ⊢
        exact hs)
      (SupportClosure.self (#"p")))
have hhand : HandedSupportClosure [bad] (#"p") :=
  (h.1 [bad] (#"p") hleft hA).mp hsc
have hseq : [bad] ⇒ #"p" :=
  (thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048 [bad] (#"p")).mp hhand
have heq : bad = #"p" :=
  (thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011 (A := bad) (s := "p")).mp hseq
simp [bad] at heq
op_000059.counterexample_text: Concrete refutation: set Γ = [#"p" ⧹ #"p"] and A = #"p". Then the left-only assumptions are satisfied, `SupportClosure Γ A` holds, but `HandedSupportClosure Γ A` does not.

op_000063.source_statement: Define a self-contained branching residual automaton on atomic states: from `(Γ, s)`, each division candidate yields the continuation state together with the residual state obtained by residualizing its argument premise. Prove that `Γ ⇒ A` iff the initial state returned by `residualAtomicState Γ A` is accepted by this internal branching automaton.
op_000063.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (Γ ⇒ A) ↔
    ∃ Δ s, residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) ∧
      AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (Δ, s)
op_000063.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a ternary branching step so each candidate carries both the continuation state and the residualized argument state, then stated the exactness theorem using the same residualAtomicState root interface as the existing graph theorem.

op_000063.proof_sketch: This is the same strong-induction proof pattern as `thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062`; the only new ingredient is that a branching step carries a second child witnessing the residualized argument premise. Induct on `list_degree Γ + tp_degree A`. For atomic goals `A = # s`, use `thm_atom_goal_candidate_cases_000007`: the base case `Γ = [# s]` gives `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts.base`, and each candidate case produces a continuation sequent plus an argument sequent `Δ ⇒ A'`; the continuation is smaller by the same degree inequality used in `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030`, while the argument branch is handled by the induction hypothesis applied to the witness `residualAtomicState Δ A' = some arg`, yielding `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts.step`. For the reverse direction, induct on `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (Δ, s)`: `base` is `Sequent.ax`, and in a `step` the continuation child gives the atomic continuation sequent by IH while the argument child, together with its residual witness, gives `Δ ⇒ A'` by the smaller-instance induction hypothesis; then close with `Sequent.rdiv_l` or `Sequent.ldiv_l`. For `ldiv` and `rdiv` outer goals, `residualAtomicState` just peels the top connective, so the induction hypothesis combines with `ldiv_invertible`/`rdiv_invertible`, `Sequent.ldiv_r`/`Sequent.rdiv_r`, and the empty-context `none` cases match `nonempty_premises`.

op_000063.source_statement: Define a self-contained branching residual automaton on atomic states: from `(Γ, s)`, each division candidate yields the continuation state together with the residual state obtained by residualizing its argument premise. Prove that `Γ ⇒ A` iff the initial state returned by `residualAtomicState Γ A` is accepted by this internal branching automaton.
op_000063.formalized_statement:
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp),
  (Γ ⇒ A) ↔
    ∃ Δ s, residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) ∧
      AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (Δ, s)

op_000063.proof_sketch: This should be a proof. The smallest missing bridge is `forall Delta s, AtomicCandidateTree Delta s <-> AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (Delta, s)`. For `AtomicCandidateTree Delta s -> AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts (Delta, s)`, do strong induction on `list_degree Delta`: in a `step_rdiv` or `step_ldiv`, use `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` and the first half of `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050` on the premise sequent `Delta0 => A0` to obtain `residualAtomicState Delta0 A0 = some arg` together with an atomic candidate tree on `arg`, and both recursive states are smaller by the same `candidates_list_degree` degree gap used in `thm_atomic_candidate_tree_iff_sequent_000030` plus the deterministic peeling in `residualAtomicState`. For the reverse implication, induct on `AtomicResidualBranchingAccepts`: the continuation child gives the recursive atomic tree directly, while the argument child and its residual witness are converted back to `Delta0 => A0` using `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050` and `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048`, so `AtomicCandidateTree.step_rdiv` or `AtomicCandidateTree.step_ldiv` applies. Then rewrite `(Gamma => A)` by `thm_handed_support_closure_iff_sequent_000048` and the first half of `thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050`, and replace `AtomicCandidateTree` with the bridge to get exactly the claimed residual-branching acceptance criterion.

mt_main_theorem.proof_sketch: The statement is false. Use the existing same-handed counterexample `bad := #"p" ⧹ #"p"`. Its residual atomic state at goal `#"p"` is `some ([bad], "p")`; the residual context is left-only and contains an occurrence of `"p"`, but graph acceptance would imply `[bad] ⇒ #"p"`, contradicting the verified singleton-atom sequent characterization.
mt_main_theorem.counterexample_text: Take `bad := #"p" ⧹ #"p"`, `Γ = [bad]`, `A = #"p"`, `Δ = [bad]`, and `s = "p"`. Then `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)` by simplification. Also `(∀ x ∈ Δ, isLeftOnly x)` holds, since `bad` is left-only, so the same-handed premise is satisfied. The right side `∃ B ∈ Δ, occurs_atom s B` holds with `B = bad`, because `occurs_atom "p" (#"p" ⧹ #"p")` is true. But `AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)` is false: otherwise, using `thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062` together with the residual-state equality, we would obtain `[bad] ⇒ #"p"`; then `thm_singleton_atomic_sequent_iff_000011` would force `bad = #"p"`, contradiction. So for this residual state, occurrence holds while acceptance fails.

mt_main_theorem.proof_sketch: Use the residual support normal form for `SupportClosure`, then split on whether the unique residual atomic state is graph-accepting. Acceptance gives derivability via residual graph recognition; non-acceptance gives the second disjunct. Conversely, derivability implies support by `thm_derivable_support_ok_000032`, and an explicit residual witness gives `SupportClosure` directly.
mt_main_theorem.proof_text:
intro Γ A
constructor
· intro hsup
  rcases ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 Γ A).2).mp hsup with ⟨Δ, s, hres, hocc⟩
  by_cases hacc : AtomicResidualGraphAccepts (Δ, s)
  · left
    exact (thm_residual_graph_recognizes_sequent_000062 Γ A).2 ⟨Δ, s, hres, hacc⟩
  · right
    exact ⟨Δ, s, hres, hocc, hacc⟩
· intro h
  rcases h with hseq | ⟨Δ, s, hres, hocc, _hacc⟩
  · rw [thm_support_closure_matches_support_ok_000036]
    exact thm_derivable_support_ok_000032 hseq
  · exact ((thm_residual_support_normal_form_000050 Γ A).2).mpr ⟨Δ, s, hres, hocc⟩

op_000065.source_statement: For any Γ A Δ s, if residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s), then every state reachable in the residual graph from (Δ, s) has the form (Δ', s) with every formula in Δ' a subformula of some formula in Γ ++ [A].
op_000065.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {s : String}, residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) → ∀ p : AtomicResidualState, Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (Δ, s) p → ∃ Δ' : List Tp, p = (Δ', s) ∧ ∀ B ∈ Δ', ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C
op_000065.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized reachability as the reflexive-transitive closure `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep`, so the start state is included and every reachable state is required to preserve the same atom label `s`.

op_000065.proof_sketch: Proceed by induction on the reachability proof `Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep (Δ, s) p`. In the reflexive base case, apply `thm_residual_state_subformula_occurs_000061` to the hypothesis `residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s)`; this gives exactly that every `B ∈ Δ` is a subformula of some formula in `Γ ++ [A]`, and the state is `(Δ, s)`. For the step case, first use the induction hypothesis on the predecessor state `(Δ0, s)` to write it as `(Δ0', s)` with the desired support property, then case on the last `AtomicResidualGraphStep`. In either constructor, `candidates_list_degree` shows the predecessor context decomposes as either `L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ` or `Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R`, so every formula in the successor context is either unchanged from the predecessor or is the exposed formula `B`. Unchanged formulas keep their original witness, and for the new `B` one uses `Subformula.rdiv_left (Subformula.refl B)` or `Subformula.ldiv_right (Subformula.refl B)` together with a small transitivity lemma for `Subformula` to compose with the predecessor witness, so the label `s` is preserved and the support invariant propagates to every reachable state.

op_000065.proof_sketch: Induct on the `Relation.ReflTransGen` reachability derivation. The base case is exactly the initial residual-state subformula theorem. In the step case, case on the final `AtomicResidualGraphStep`; inherited formulas use the induction hypothesis, while the newly exposed formula uses the predecessor witness for the principal division formula together with `subformula_trans` and `Subformula.rdiv_left` or `Subformula.ldiv_right`.
op_000065.prelude_code:
lemma subformula_trans {A B C : Tp} : Subformula A B → Subformula B C → Subformula A C := by
  intro hAB hBC
  induction hBC with
  | refl => exact hAB
  | ldiv_left h ih => exact Subformula.ldiv_left ih
  | ldiv_right h ih => exact Subformula.ldiv_right ih
  | rdiv_left h ih => exact Subformula.rdiv_left ih
  | rdiv_right h ih => exact Subformula.rdiv_right ih
op_000065.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A s q hres hreach
have hstart : ∀ B ∈ Δ, ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C :=
  (thm_residual_state_subformula_occurs_000061 (Γ := Γ) (Δ := Δ) (A := A) (s := s) hres).1
induction hreach with
| refl =>
    constructor
    · rfl
    · simpa using hstart
| tail hreach hstep ih =>
    rcases ih with ⟨hs, hsub⟩
    cases hstep with
    | rdiv Γ0 L Δ0 Λ A0 B0 s0 hc harg =>
        constructor
        · exact hs
        · intro X hX
          have hctx : Γ0 = L ++ [B0 ⧸ A0] ++ Δ0 ++ Λ :=
            cand_rdiv_context_eq (Γ := Γ0) (L := L) (Δ := Δ0) (Λ := Λ) (A := A0) (B := B0) hc
          have hcases : X ∈ L ∨ X = B0 ∨ X ∈ Λ := by
            simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
          rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
          · have hmem : X ∈ Γ0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem
          · have hmem : Tp.rdiv X A0 ∈ Γ0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append]
            rcases hsub (Tp.rdiv X A0) hmem with ⟨C, hC, hSC⟩
            exact ⟨C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.rdiv_left (Subformula.refl X)) hSC⟩
          · have hmem : X ∈ Γ0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem
    | ldiv Γ0 Γ1 Δ0 R A0 B0 s0 hc harg =>
        constructor
        · exact hs
        · intro X hX
          have hctx : Γ0 = Γ1 ++ Δ0 ++ [A0 ⧹ B0] ++ R := by
            symm
            simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ0) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ1 Δ0 A0 B0 R) hc)
          have hcases : X ∈ Γ1 ∨ X = B0 ∨ X ∈ R := by
            simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hX
          rcases hcases with hX | rfl | hX
          · have hmem : X ∈ Γ0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem
          · have hmem : Tp.ldiv A0 X ∈ Γ0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append]
            rcases hsub (Tp.ldiv A0 X) hmem with ⟨C, hC, hSC⟩
            exact ⟨C, hC, subformula_trans (Subformula.ldiv_right (Subformula.refl X)) hSC⟩
          · have hmem : X ∈ Γ0 := by
              rw [hctx]
              simp [List.mem_append, hX]
            exact hsub X hmem

First, one must prove a closure lemma saying every continuation/argument state used by the internal branching presentation still satisfies the root-relative predicate `ResidualStateSubformulaBound Γ A`; this is essentially the missing global reachable-state control behind open problem `op_000065`. Second, one must turn that bound into an actual `Finset` of states for `FiniteAtomicBranchingAutomaton`; no finite-enumeration machinery for bounded residual states is present, and building it from scratch requires a sizeable new development. Although `thm_residual_branching_automaton_correct_000063` exists in `Scratch.loop.lean`, it is not part of the imported context, and even after recreating it locally the finite packaging step remains unproved.
