op_000066.proof_sketch: Reuse the derived reachable-subformula invariant, then bound reachable contexts by finite subformula support and by the degree-decreasing residual step relation. This is the same argument already verified in `Scratch.loop.lean`, inlined here using helper lemmas because the proof harness imports `Generated.Manifest` and `Derived`, not `Scratch.loop`.
op_000066.prelude_code:
lemma subformula_set_finite (C : Tp) : Set.Finite {B : Tp | Subformula B C} := by
  induction C with
  | atom s =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton (# s)).subset ?_
      intro B hB
      cases hB
      simp
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (A ⧹ B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | ldiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | ldiv_right h =>
          simp [h]
  | rdiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (A ⧸ B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | rdiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | rdiv_right h =>
          simp [h]

lemma subformula_support_finite (src : List Tp) :
    Set.Finite {B : Tp | ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C} := by
  induction src with
  | nil =>
      simpa using (Set.finite_empty : Set.Finite (∅ : Set Tp))
  | cons C src ih =>
      rw [show ({B : Tp | ∃ D ∈ C :: src, Subformula B D} : Set Tp) =
          {B : Tp | Subformula B C} ∪ {B : Tp | ∃ D ∈ src, Subformula B D} by
        ext B
        simp]
      exact (subformula_set_finite C).union ih

lemma finite_lists_bounded_of_finite {α : Type*} {S : Set α} (hS : S.Finite) :
    ∀ N : Nat,
      Set.Finite {l : List α | l.length ≤ N ∧ ∀ a ∈ l, a ∈ S}
  | 0 =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton ([] : List α)).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      have hl0 : l.length = 0 := Nat.eq_zero_of_le_zero hl.1
      have hnil : l = [] := by
        rwa [List.length_eq_zero_iff] at hl0
      simpa [hnil]
  | N + 1 =>
      let T : Set (List α) := {l | l.length ≤ N ∧ ∀ a ∈ l, a ∈ S}
      have hT : T.Finite := finite_lists_bounded_of_finite hS N
      refine ((Set.finite_singleton ([] : List α)).union
        (Set.Finite.image (fun p : α × List α => p.1 :: p.2) (hS.prod hT))).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      cases l with
      | nil =>
          simp
      | cons a tl =>
          right
          refine ⟨(a, tl), ?_, rfl⟩
          constructor
          · exact hl.2 a (by simp)
          · change tl.length ≤ N ∧ ∀ b ∈ tl, b ∈ S
            constructor
            · simpa using hl.1
            · intro b hb
              exact hl.2 b (by simp [hb])

lemma tp_degree_pos (A : Tp) : 0 < tp_degree A := by
  cases A <;> simp [tp_degree]

lemma list_length_le_list_degree : ∀ Γ : List Tp, Γ.length ≤ list_degree Γ
  | [] => by simp [list_degree]
  | A :: Γ => by
      have hA : 1 ≤ tp_degree A := Nat.succ_le_of_lt (tp_degree_pos A)
      simpa [list_degree] using Nat.add_le_add hA (list_length_le_list_degree Γ)

lemma atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 := by
  cases h with
  | rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg =>
      have hΓ : Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ :=
        cand_rdiv_context_eq (Γ := Γ) (L := L) (Δ := Δ) (Λ := Λ) (A := A) (B := B) hc
      rw [hΓ]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega
  | ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg =>
      have hΓ : Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R) hc)
      rw [hΓ]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega

lemma residual_reachable_degree_le {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 ≤ list_degree p.1 := by
  induction h with
  | refl =>
      exact le_rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      exact le_trans (atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep).le ih
op_000066.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A s hres
have hsubsrc : Set.Finite {B : Tp | ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  subformula_support_finite (Γ ++ [A])
have hctxfin :
    Set.Finite {ctx : List Tp |
      ctx.length ≤ list_degree Δ ∧
      ∀ B ∈ ctx, ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  finite_lists_bounded_of_finite
    (S := {B : Tp | ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C})
    hsubsrc (list_degree Δ)
refine (Set.Finite.image (fun ctx : List Tp => (ctx, s)) hctxfin).subset ?_
intro q hq
rcases thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065 Γ Δ A s q hres hq with
  ⟨hs, hsub⟩
have hdeg : list_degree q.1 ≤ list_degree Δ :=
  residual_reachable_degree_le hq
refine ⟨q.1, ?_, ?_⟩
· exact ⟨le_trans (list_length_le_list_degree q.1) hdeg, hsub⟩
· cases q
  simpa [hs]

op_000068.formalized_statement:
∀ (src : List Tp) (p q : AtomicResidualState),

op_000071.proof_sketch: Use the reachable-state invariant from `thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065`, add a local lemma that each residual step strictly decreases `list_degree`, deduce every reachable context has degree and hence length bounded by `list_degree Δ`, and combine this with finiteness of the source subformula set and finiteness of bounded-length lists over a finite alphabet.
op_000071.prelude_code:
lemma subformula_set_finite (C : Tp) : Set.Finite {B : Tp | Subformula B C} := by
  induction C with
  | atom s =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton (Tp.atom s)).subset ?_
      intro B hB
      cases hB
      simp
  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (Tp.ldiv A B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | ldiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | ldiv_right h =>
          simp [h]
  | rdiv A B ihA ihB =>
      refine ((ihA.union ihB).union (Set.finite_singleton (Tp.rdiv A B))).subset ?_
      intro X hX
      cases hX with
      | refl =>
          simp
      | rdiv_left h =>
          simp [h]
      | rdiv_right h =>
          simp [h]

lemma subformula_support_finite (src : List Tp) :
    Set.Finite {B : Tp | ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C} := by
  induction src with
  | nil =>
      simpa using (Set.finite_empty : Set.Finite (∅ : Set Tp))
  | cons C src ih =>
      rw [show ({B : Tp | ∃ D ∈ C :: src, Subformula B D} : Set Tp) =
          {B : Tp | Subformula B C} ∪ {B : Tp | ∃ D ∈ src, Subformula B D} by
        ext B
        simp]
      exact (subformula_set_finite C).union ih

lemma finite_lists_bounded_of_finite {α : Type*} {S : Set α} (hS : S.Finite) :
    ∀ N : Nat,
      Set.Finite {l : List α | l.length ≤ N ∧ ∀ a ∈ l, a ∈ S} := by
  intro N
  induction N with
  | zero =>
      refine (Set.finite_singleton ([] : List α)).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      cases l with
      | nil =>
          simp
      | cons a tl =>
          simp at hl
  | succ N ih =>
      let T : Set (List α) := {l | l.length ≤ N ∧ ∀ a ∈ l, a ∈ S}
      have hT : T.Finite := ih
      refine ((Set.finite_singleton ([] : List α)).union
        (Set.Finite.image (fun p : α × List α => p.1 :: p.2) (hS.prod hT))).subset ?_
      intro l hl
      cases l with
      | nil =>
          simp
      | cons a tl =>
          right
          refine ⟨(a, tl), ?_, rfl⟩
          constructor
          · exact hl.2 a (by simp)
          · change tl.length ≤ N ∧ ∀ b ∈ tl, b ∈ S
            constructor
            · simpa using hl.1
            · intro b hb
              exact hl.2 b (by simp [hb])

lemma list_length_le_list_degree : ∀ Γ : List Tp, Γ.length ≤ list_degree Γ
  | [] => by
      simp [list_degree]
  | A :: Γ => by
      have hA : 1 ≤ tp_degree A := by
        cases A <;> simp [tp_degree]
      have hΓ : Γ.length ≤ list_degree Γ := list_length_le_list_degree Γ
      calc
        (A :: Γ).length = Γ.length + 1 := by simp
        _ = 1 + Γ.length := by omega
        _ ≤ tp_degree A + list_degree Γ := Nat.add_le_add hA hΓ
        _ = list_degree (A :: Γ) := by simp [list_degree]

lemma atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1 := by
  cases h with
  | rdiv Γ L Δ Λ A B s hc harg =>
      have hΓ : Γ = L ++ [Tp.rdiv B A] ++ Δ ++ Λ := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Δ Λ) hc)
      rw [hΓ]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega
  | ldiv Γ Γ₁ Δ R A B s hc harg =>
      have hΓ : Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [Tp.ldiv A B] ++ R := by
        symm
        simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Γ := Γ) (c := Cand.ldiv Γ₁ Δ A B R) hc)
      rw [hΓ]
      simp [list_degree_traversible, List.append_assoc, list_degree, tp_degree]
      omega

lemma residual_reachable_degree_le {p q : AtomicResidualState}
    (h : Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q) :
    list_degree q.1 ≤ list_degree p.1 := by
  induction h with
  | refl =>
      exact le_rfl
  | tail hreach hstep ih =>
      exact le_trans (atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep).le ih

intro Γ Δ A s hres
have hsubsrc : Set.Finite {B : Tp | ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  subformula_support_finite (Γ ++ [A])
have hctxfin :
    Set.Finite {ctx : List Tp |
      ctx.length ≤ list_degree Δ ∧
      ∀ B ∈ ctx, ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C} :=
  finite_lists_bounded_of_finite
    (S := {B : Tp | ∃ C ∈ Γ ++ [A], Subformula B C})
    hsubsrc (list_degree Δ)
refine (Set.Finite.image (fun ctx : List Tp => (ctx, s)) hctxfin).subset ?_
intro q hq
rcases thm_residual_reachable_subformula_invariant_000065 Γ Δ A s q hres hq with
  ⟨hs, hsub⟩
have hdeg : list_degree q.1 ≤ list_degree Δ :=
  residual_reachable_degree_le hq
refine ⟨q.1, ?_, ?_⟩
· exact ⟨le_trans (list_length_le_list_degree q.1) hdeg, hsub⟩
· cases q
  simpa using hs.symm

op_000075.proof_sketch: The statement is false. Specialize the claimed finiteness to `src = [# "a"]` and `s = "a"`. Because `Subformula` is reflexive, every state `(List.replicate n (# "a"), "a")` lies in the resulting set: every formula occurring in the first component is exactly `# "a"`, hence a subformula of the unique source formula. This yields an injective map `Nat → S`. If `S` were finite, then the subtype `S` would be a finite type, and `Finite.of_injective` would imply that `Nat` is finite, contradiction.
op_000075.proof_text:
intro h
let S : Set AtomicResidualState :=
  { p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = "a" ∧ ∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ [# "a"], Subformula B C }
have hSfin : S.Finite := by
  simpa [S] using h [# "a"] "a"
let f : ℕ → S := fun n =>
  ⟨(List.replicate n (# "a"), "a"), by
    constructor
    · rfl
    · intro B hB
      refine ⟨# "a", by simp, ?_⟩
      have hEq : B = # "a" := eq_of_mem_replicate hB
      simpa [hEq] using (Subformula.refl (# "a"))⟩
letI : Fintype S := hSfin.fintype
have hf : Function.Injective f := by
  intro m n hmn
  simpa [f] using congr_arg (fun x : S => x.1.1.length) hmn
haveI : Finite ℕ := Finite.of_injective f hf
exact not_finite ℕ
op_000075.counterexample_text: Take `src = [# "a"]` and `s = "a"`. For each `n : Nat`, the state `p_n = (List.replicate n (# "a"), "a")` satisfies the defining property, since every `B ∈ p_n.1` is equal to `# "a"`, and `Subformula.refl` gives `Subformula (# "a") (# "a")`. The states `p_n` are pairwise distinct because their first components have different lengths, so this same-atom subformula-bounded class is infinite.

op_000084.source_statement: For every finite `src` and atom `s`, a subset of `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ∧ (∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) }` is finite if and only if the values `list_degree p.1` are uniformly bounded on that subset.
op_000084.formalized_statement: ∀ (src : List Tp) (s : String) (S : Set AtomicResidualState), S ⊆ { p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ∧ (∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) } → (S.Finite ↔ ∃ N : Nat, ∀ p ∈ S, list_degree p.1 ≤ N)
op_000084.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the unspecified subset as a set `S` contained in the ambient same-atom subformula state set; `src` is taken finite via the repository’s `List Tp` representation.

op_000084.proof_sketch: Forward: the image of a finite set `S` under `p ↦ list_degree p.1` is a finite set of naturals, hence bounded above by `Set.finite_iff_bddAbove`. Reverse: if all `list_degree p.1` are bounded by `N`, then `S` is contained in the finite degree-bounded same-atom subformula slice from `thm_degree_bounded_subformula_slice_finite_000072`.
op_000084.proof_text:
intro src s S hSub
constructor
· intro hfin
  have hfinImg :
      Set.Finite ((fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1) '' S) :=
    Set.Finite.image (fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1) hfin
  rcases (Set.finite_iff_bddAbove.mp hfinImg) with ⟨N, hN⟩
  refine ⟨N, ?_⟩
  intro p hp
  exact hN ⟨p, hp, rfl⟩
· rintro ⟨N, hN⟩
  refine (thm_degree_bounded_subformula_slice_finite_000072 src s N).subset ?_
  intro p hp
  have hp' := hSub hp
  exact ⟨hp'.1, hN p hp, hp'.2⟩

op_000078.formalized_statement: WellFounded (fun q p : AtomicResidualState => AtomicResidualGraphStep p q)
op_000078.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized well-foundedness using the converse relation, since `AtomicResidualGraphStep p q` is a forward step from `p` to the smaller state `q`, while `WellFounded` is stated on predecessor relations.

op_000078.proof_sketch: Use `list_degree` on the residual context as a measure. The lemma `atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt` shows that any step `AtomicResidualGraphStep p q` gives `list_degree q.1 < list_degree p.1`, so the reversed step relation is a subrelation of `measure (fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1)`, hence well-founded.
op_000078.proof_text:
refine Subrelation.wf ?_ (measure (fun p : AtomicResidualState => list_degree p.1)).wf
intro q p hstep
simpa using atomicResidualGraphStep_degree_lt hstep
